name: Singapore
intro: Singapore treats professional certification the way it treats everything else: as infrastructure. SkillsFuture credits, employer training budgets and a government that benchmarks workforce credentials have made PMP and Scrum certifications near-standard for delivery roles in the city-state's banks, tech regional headquarters and logistics giants. Competition is regional — your CV sits next to candidates from across ASEAN — so the credential is often the filter that decides who interviews. Our live online weekend batch runs on the IST schedule, which lands mid-morning to evening in Singapore: the full workshop fits in one weekend with no annual leave spent. The cohort is international and instruction is case-driven, echoing the multi-market programmes Singapore professionals typically run. Afterwards we support your exam application, provide full-length mock exams, and keep the trainer accessible for questions until you certify.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Singapore?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Singapore?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Singapore.
